title: TUI message_preview uses str() on timestamp which may produce unreadable output
description:
File: python/src/lichen/tui/native.py
Lines: 389-400

The message_preview function converts timestamps using str():

    def message_preview(record: MessageRecord, *, unread: bool = False) -> MessagePreview:
        target = record.sender or record.recipient or "--"
        age = str(record.received or record.timestamp or "--")
        ...

If record.received or record.timestamp is a datetime object, str() produces:
    "2026-07-11 14:30:45.123456"

This is 26 characters, which doesn't fit well in the age field (line 380 uses {:>5} which expects ~5 chars).

If it's an integer timestamp, str() produces the raw number which is also not human-friendly.

Impact: Age column in message list may be unreadable or overflow its allocated space.

Fix: Format timestamps appropriately:
    if isinstance(ts := (record.received or record.timestamp), datetime):
        age = ts.strftime("%H:%M")
    elif isinstance(ts, int | float):
        # Assume seconds since epoch
        age = datetime.fromtimestamp(ts).strftime("%H:%M")
    else:
        age = str(ts) if ts else "--"
